title: Blu ray and DVD Localization
wikitext:
[]: Blu-ray & DVD Localization             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Thu Jun 23, 2011 6:02 pm In 1.11, Misato & Ritsuko briefly discuss the unlikely repairs to building B, Misato complains: “Bum” is a very British word used for that particular area of the human body, so it makes sense that this is a screen cap taken from the UK release of 1.11 Can I assume that the US subtitles used “Butt” in place of “Bum” for this scene, or did Funimation opt for “ba-dunk-a-dunk” instead? You’d think nothing would need changing, right? Brits DO know what a butt is afterall. It’s just a funny thought to me. I’m curious, do the US subtitles for 2.22 match the UK subs for Mari’s line here?: Is a screencap of Mari a   SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Thu Jun 23, 2011 6:09 pm It does say butt instead (infact, I think the English dub says that too). And (at least in the dub) instead of saying "doggy", she says "puppy". And, I'm guessing "mum's the word" means "I got to go", right?

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Jun 23, 2011 6:16 pm Oh man, this is priceless. Someone needs to take this to its logical extreme.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Thu Jun 23, 2011 9:25 pm Stryker wrote: And, I'm guessing "mum's the word" means "I got to go", right?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jun 23, 2011 9:35 pm I forget if this was a personal translation or Funimation's official translation, but I did see "Mum's the word" for Mari's phrase to Shinji somewhere for that 2.22 scene. I don't have my 1.11 or 2.22 DVDs anymore (sold them to a friend so I could save up for the Japanese BDs) so I can't check.

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Thu Jun 23, 2011 9:48 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Thu Jun 23, 2011 10:01 pm Seele00TextOnly wrote: If only there was also a British-English dub for us to enjoy alongside the American-English dub.

[]:             Quote Post by gatotsu911 » Thu Jun 23, 2011 11:53 pm Are there a lot of dedicated voice actors in the UK? What do they do?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jun 24, 2011 12:00 am gatotsu911 wrote: Are there a lot of dedicated voice actors in the UK? What do they do?

[]:             Quote Post by Wadels » Fri Jun 24, 2011 2:25 am I know a few video games are re-dubbed in the UK with British voice actors instead of retaining the American voices. The Ape Escape series is a good example of this.

[]:             Quote Post by chazthesilencer » Fri Jun 24, 2011 4:39 am Alright, which one of you smart-arse mods replaced the thread title’s “locali s ation” with “locali z ation”? :] gatotsu911 wrote: Someone needs to take this to its logical extreme.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jun 24, 2011 5:03 am chazthesilencer wrote: Alright, which one of you smart-arse mods replaced the thread title’s “locali s ation” with “locali z ation”? :]

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jun 24, 2011 8:44 am chazthesilencer wrote: Alright, which one of you smart-arse mods replaced the thread title’s “locali s ation” with “locali z ation”? :]

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jun 24, 2011 8:55 am Monocle-Mari... I laughed so hard.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Fri Jun 24, 2011 9:21 am chazthesilencer wrote: Alright, which one of you smart-arse mods replaced the thread title’s “locali s ation” with “locali z ation”? :] More Mari caps   SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jun 24, 2011 10:42 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Problem?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jun 24, 2011 11:51 am symbv wrote: What is the problem with British English?

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Fri Jun 24, 2011 12:20 pm The entry in Wikipedia starts with "Localization or localisation,..."

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Jun 24, 2011 12:48 pm symbv wrote: The entry in Wikipedia starts with "Localization or localisation,..."

[]:             Quote Post by Tarnsman » Fri Jun 24, 2011 1:47 pm I checked my Funimation subs and found no British phrases, also Mari says puppy and nervling, in the sub/dub respectively.

You’d think nothing would need changing, right? Brits DO know what a butt is afterall. It’s just a funny thought to me.
